docs.title: Technical Metadata
docs.text:
Technical metadata refers to the descriptive information about data that helps to understand its technical characteristics, structure, and relationships. It is useful to include technical metadata with certain file types to provide more specific information.

The new Ingest system enables depositors to utilise a single Core Metadata template to upload their files to the system. However, for some data categories additional technical metadata is required. Below is a full list of the data categories that require additional technical metadata file to be deposited alongside your collection.

docs.title: How to upload your technical metadata
docs.text:
To upload Technical metadata files, include them in the folder of files that you want to deposit and have uploaded on the Files page when you submit a new collection.

It is not required to list these technical metadata files as a separate row in the Core Metadata Template, however, each file (with an appropriate file extension) should be listed in the Technical Metadata column of the Core Metadata template. If you fail to include a required Technical metadata file, or include the file but do not list it in the Core metadata template, then Ingest will produce an error.

docs.title: Data types that require technical metadata
docs.text:
The table below provides a list of templates that can be used for required Technical metadata. It is not mandatory to use these templates but they provide a useful starting point for the types of information that we would like to collect for some Data Categories.

Each template contains sample data for each field . These templates can be used and amended as necessary to suit the needs of your collection. For a full list of the fields required for each technical metadata template please the technical metadata field list.

Data Category File Extensions Technical Metadata Template Audiovisual acc, aif, au, avi, bwf, flack, mkv, mp3, mp4, mpg, avg, wav, mpeg Audiovisual metadata template Geographic Information System (GIS) Data adf, asc, csv, e00, geojson, gml, img, jpg, kml, mid, png, shp, tif GIS Metadata template Geophysics csv, dat, mgr, rep, sgy, txt, xcp Geophysical survey metadata template Geophysics (Proprietary) Any file types Geophysical survey metadata template Laser Scanning tif, txt, e57, las, obj, pts 3D metadata template Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) tif, txt, e57, las, obj, pts 3D metadata template Photogrammetric Survey dng, jpg, obj, pts, tiff, wrl 3D metadata template Tabular Data accdb, csv, dbf, json, mdb, odb, ods, rdf, sql, tsv, txt, xls, xlsx, xml Tabular Data technical metadata Vector Graphic dwg, dxf, svg Vector technical metadata Virtual Reality and Visualisation obj, stl, wrl 3D metadata template

docs.title: Template Download
docs.text:
A .xlsx version of the Core Metadata Template can be downloaded from the following link:

Download Template

The template can also be downloaded from the Files menu when depositing using the Ingest system.

The Core Metadata Template contains example data to assist in completing the form (written in red). Please delete this data before uploading the template to Ingest.

docs.title: Costing Calculator
docs.text:
The Costing Calculator is accessed via the top right hand corner of the Dashboard. This costing calculator can be used to generate a quotation for depositing a dataset via Ingest.

To build a quote:

  1. First, enter the total size of the collection in GB. This should be the size of all files that you wish to deposit.
  2. Select the Data Category that you want to deposit. A full list of data categories accepted by Ingest can be found in the Data Category pages.
  3. Select the file extension for those files from the drop down list.
  4. Enter the number of files for that category (as well as any other information relevant to that specific category).
  5. Once complete click the ‘Add to Quote’ button’.
  6. Repeat Step 2 for all files that you wish to deposit.

A breakdown of the fees can be found on the Costs page.

To download a copy of your quotation click the ‘Download’ button on the bottom right hand corner of the page. This will allow you to download a pdf version of the costings calculated on this page that you can share with your organisation.

Reset the Calculator using the button in the bottom right hand corner of the page.

Please note, for collections exceeding 100,000 files or 1Tb of data, for large multifaceted infrastructure projects, or projects with special interface requirements, please contact us for costing advice and guidance.

docs.title: Collections List
docs.text: The Collections List is accessed via the toolbar along the top of the page. This menu provides a full list of any active or submitted collections undertaken by you or by someone in your organisation.

docs.title: Filters
docs.text: Navigate your collections using the filters or the search bar at the top of the page . Click ‘Open’, ‘Submitted’ or ‘Accessioned’ to filter your collections by its Status. Click 'Only show my collections' to filter the list to collections you are working on rather than entries for your organisation as a whole.

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
The status of the collection, located at the end of each row, will determine what you can do with the collection.

  • Open - You have started entering information about your collection but have not yet submitted.
  • Submitted - You have completed and submitted your collection to one of our archivists, who is now checking that the data and metadata meets our accession requirements.
  • Accessioned - The deposition is now complete and is now accessioned into our archives. The data will now be archived into our collections and will soon be visible via our catalogues.

Click the next to each field name to open a help menu and find out more about what the field means.

Click the ‘View’ button at the end of each row to open the Collection page, view your input and save new information.

If you wish to make a change to a collection that has already been submitted, you will need to request that it be reopened by one of our archivists. This can be done by sending a Message or contacting the Helpdesk.

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text:
The Dashboard is the default homepage when you sign in to Ingest.

The bar chart provides a graphical interface of the collections that you have started working on, organised by status.

Click a bar in the chart to go to the Collections List page, filtered by Collection Status.

Click the green button at the top of the page to start a ‘New Collection’.

If you have any collections that have been started but have remained inactive for a long period of time, then they will be listed on the Dashboard page. These collections will be automatically deleted after 12 months of inactivity.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: This online guidance provides detailed instructions for all user types, from first-time depositors to system administrators. Whether you are preparing to archive data for the first time, or have archived with us before, these guides will help you efficiently navigate the Ingest system.

docs.title: How to Use This Guide
docs.text: This online resource serves as a comprehensive user guide to using Ingest, helping you to navigate each of the features and menus and provide examples for good practice in completing each field. We recommend you navigate through the guidance step by step, building from basic concepts to more advanced features, to help guide you through the deposition process.

docs.title: Ingest guides
docs.text:
• Getting Started

    Register, navigate your dashboard and start a deposition

    Introduction

  • New Collections

    Submit collection information, incl. projects, people and resources.

    Submitting a New Collection

  • Core Metadata Template

    Download templates and explore field guidelines.

    Metadata instructions

  • Training

    Find out about upcoming sessions, Q&A discussions, and share feedback.

    Upcoming Sessions

docs.title: Navigation features
docs.text:
• Use the navigation tabs above to explore each section of the guidance.
  • Click headings in the sidebar to navigate to topics more quickly.
  • Use the search bar at the top of the page to find specific topics.
  • On mobile devices, tap the menu icon (☰) in the top left hand corner to access the navigation pane.

Pro tip

Use the search function at the top of the page to quickly jump to any topic across the entire guide.

docs.title: Improvements
docs.text: If you have any recommendations for improvements or additions to this documentation please contact the Helpdesk with the subject line 'Ingest Guidance'.

docs.title: Messages
docs.text:
Use the Messages menu to communicate with the archivists who are processing your collections, as well as any collaborators who may be working on your collections. This could include messages about multiple collections.

Recipients: Search for a recipient using their name or email address. The menu will autocomplete a recipient from an approved list.

Subject: Add a subject line, just like an email. Make it as clear as possible so that the archivist can best help you.

Message: Write your full request in the message field. Include as much information as possible so that the archivist can best help you.

All of your messages will appear on the top of this page after you send them. You can reply to each thread separately to organise these communications.

docs.title: How to register
docs.text: To use Ingest, you will first need to register via the User Management Application (UMA). You can access this system on the Ingest homepage by clicking 'Create an Account' under the login panel.

docs.title: User Management Application
docs.text:
UMA is a centralised platform for managing user accounts and access permissions across ADS and HSDS services. You can use UMA to manage your own personal data and, if an Admin user, manage users for your organisation.

To register, enter your email address and complete the reCAPTCHA verification. Then enter your details, including a password. You can also add (or create) an ORCID. You will need to accept the Terms and Conditions and privacy policy and press "save details". After submitting your registration, you'll receive a verification email. Check your inbox and click the verification link to activate your account.

For more information see the UMA help pages.

Once registered for UMA you will be able to manage your account and login to Ingest using the same login credentials.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
The Settings menu is accessed via the top right hand corner of the Dashboard. In this menu you can set default information about Copyright Holders, Web Contact and Invoice details.

By inputting this information into the Settings menu you ensure that you do not have to re-enter these details for every collection. The default options can also be edited within these pages if required. Follow the links above to find out further information about each field.

docs.title: Default Copyright Holder
docs.text:
Please enter details about who is the default copyright holder of your collections. This may include the original author or the organisation who is responsible for creating the content. If you have multiple copyright holders, additional information can be added on the People & Organisations page.

To add details about the copyright holder first select the ‘Type’ - either a ‘Person’ or ‘Organisation’.

If you selected a person you will need to complete the following fields:

  • First Name
  • Last Name
  • ORCID - Please enter the full URL including unique sixteen digit Open Researcher and Contributor ID (ORCiD) reference, e.g. https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0000

If you selected ‘Organisation’ you will only need to enter the Organisation name.

docs.title: Default Web Contact
docs.text:
Please enter information about who users should contact if they have a question about your collection. This name and email address will be publicly available alongside your collection when it is published. A Web Contact can be a Person or an Organisation.

If you are depositing on behalf of an organisation, you may wish to include a general email address rather than the contact details of a specific person.

Please note, if you do enter the details of a specific person under web contact, please ensure you receive their permission to put their contact details on our website prior to submitting your collection.

To add details about the Web Contact first select the ‘Type’ - either a ‘Person’ or ‘Organisation’.

If you selected ‘Person’ you will need to complete the following fields:

  • First Name
  • Last Name
  • ORCID - Please enter the full URL including unique sixteen digit Open Researcher and Contributor ID (ORCiD) reference, e.g. https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0000
  • Email address

If you selected ‘Organisation’, you will need to complete the following fields:

  • Organisation Name - Type in the field to search available options. If the organisation that you are looking for does not appear, please type in the name manually.
  • Email address

docs.title: Default Invoice Details
docs.text:
Please enter contact details associated with invoicing for the cost of your deposition. You should enter the contact details of a person who is responsible for ensuring the payment of invoices so as to not delay the deposition and publication of your collection.

  • Name (first and last name)- Please enter the first and last name for your nominated contact.
  • Organisation- If applicable, please include the name of your organisation.
  • Email - Please enter the email address for your nominated contact.
  • Address - Please enter the physical address associated with your organisation or nominated contact.

docs.title: How you should structure your collection
docs.text:
Ingest has been designed to allow depositors to upload digital content and associated metadata securely to the ADS and HSDS.

Every deposit is called a Collection. A Collection is the overall archive of your work. This is the same as a collection that you will have seen in the ADS Archives.

Within each Collection, you can have one or more Projects. Projects are the different facets within a Collection. How you use projects within your collection will be determined on the scope of your work and how you would like your collection to be structured.

For example:

  • For a developer-led project it might be different phases of a fieldwork project (i.e. evaluation, excavation).
  • For a research project it might include different aspects of your funded project (i.e. database, scientific analysis)
  • For a community archaeology project it might include different aspects of community engagement (i.e. archival search, 2021 fieldwalking survey, community workshop).

If you have a very large project, such as an infrastructure project, road scheme or railway line, you can also organise your sites into a series of collections. These collections can be organised under a single umbrella collection.

If you are unsure of how to organise your collection, contact the Helpdesk for assistance.

docs.title: Collection Information
docs.text:
The first page of the deposition process is designed to gather information about the entire Collection that you are depositing. This information provided on this page provides the core collection metadata for your submission. As such, much of the information included here will be visible on the relevant archives pages.

For an explanation of the differences between a Collection and Project in Ingest, see the ‘How you should structure your collection’ information pages.

docs.title: Collection Title
docs.text:
Add a unique and descriptive title for your collection. Please note that this may be subject to change when archived. This is a mandatory field.

Example:

“Digital Archive from an Archaeological Evaluation at The Forge, The Street, Bradwell, Essex, October 2020”

docs.title: Collection Description
docs.text:
Enter a detailed description of the data collection. This will be used as the text on your collection's Introduction page in our archives. This field allows you to enter a maximum of 4000 characters. This is a mandatory field.

Example:

“This collection comprises photographs, a CAD drawing, site records and documents from an archaeological evaluation by trial trenching at The Forge, The Street, Bradwell, Essex. Work was carried out by Colchester Archaeological Trust in October 2020.

The aim of the archaeological evaluation was to record the extent of any surviving archaeological deposits, and to assess the archaeological potential of the site to allow the ECCHEA to determine if further investigation is required. A trial-trench evaluation of 2 L-shaped trenches uncovered 6 ditches, 4 undated and 2 post-medieval in date. F1-F3 in T1 are continuations of F4-6 in T2.”

docs.title: Collection Overview
docs.text: Enter a comprehensive overview of the data collection. This information could relate to the wider context of the collection or the methodologies used to collect the data. Here is an example from our archives. The text entered here will be used on the Overview page for your collection on the ADS website. It is not mandatory to include a Collection Overview but it may be useful for larger or more complex collections. This field allows you to enter a maximum of 20,000 characters.

docs.title: Collection Reuse Statement
docs.text: Please provide details about how your collection could be reused by other researchers. Please consider whether the data you are depositing may have value to other researchers and could be used in different ways than was originally intended. You may wish to highlight how your collection could serve researchers in other disciplines, how it could have an impact on a particular community or if your collection captures something rare or unique. It is not mandatory to include a Collection Reuse Statement but it can help maximise the impact of your collection and attract interdisciplinary interest. This field allows you to enter a maximum of 20,000 characters.

docs.title: Data Creation Start Date
docs.text: Enter the date when the first digital item in your collection was created. This may coincide with the start of fieldwork or a research project. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Data Creation End Date
docs.text: Enter a date when the last digital item in your collection was created. This may coincide with the end of fieldwork or a research project. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Language
docs.text: Please select the primary language used in the content of your collection. English will be selected as default. Multiple languages can be selected in this field. To remove a selection, click the ‘x’ next to the name of the language.

docs.title: Parent Collection
docs.text: If your collection is part of a larger existing collection, such as a museum collection, please select the name of the parent collection that your collection should be associated with. Please contact Helpdesk if you can not find a relevant Parent Collection on this list or if you wish to discuss creating a new Parent Collection.

docs.title: Existing Tracking ID
docs.text: If you have previously contacted the ADS or HSDS for a quote and were issued with a tracking identifier please enter it here so that we can reconcile our records.

docs.title: Embargo Date
docs.text: If you would like your collection to be restricted on a temporary basis, please select a date after which you would like the collection to be publicly available. Please note that the length of the embargo period agreed with the ADS or HSDS will depend on the sensitivities of the data involved.

docs.title: Access Licence
docs.text:
Please select the type of access licence that will determine how your collection can be shared and reused. This is a mandatory field. The ADS and HSDS strongly advocate making data as widely reusable as possible through open, permissive, re-use licences.

In the majority of cases, this will likely be a Creative Commons CC-BY 4.0 licence, which allows others to share and adapt our data as long as they provide you with the appropriate credit.

If your project has been funded by the UK Government (e.g. HS2) you will likely need to make it available via an Open Government Licence.

To read more about data licences please visit the Instructions for Depositors. If you are unsure of what licence to use please contact our Helpdesk.

docs.title: Costs
docs.text: This menu is automatically calculated based on the number of files that you are depositing within your Collection, as well as standard fees associated with any deposition.

docs.title: Explanation of fees
docs.text:
We are committed to providing open and free access to data. We therefore impose a one-off payment for deposition to safeguard the long-term future of the digital data.

Below is an explanation of the fees shown on the Costs page and what they relate to.

  • Object Fee - A fee is applied per digital object based on data category and file format. Some digital objects are charged on a sliding scale so the more you archive, the lower the cost per object. For some file types (e.g. photographs), a set number of files can be deposited for free under the general Accession Fee (discussed below). If this is the case the word ‘Free’ is shown under Cost.
  • Accession Fee - This is a standard fee that we apply to all depositions and includes a standard interface for your collection.
  • Storage Fee - This fee relates to the cost we charge for the ongoing curation of a collection. The fee is calculated per GB of uploaded data with the first 100GB included for free as part of the accession fee.

docs.title: Download a copy
docs.text:
To download a copy of your quotation click the ‘Download’ button on the top right hand corner of the page. This will allow you to download a pdf version of the costings calculated on this page that you can share with your organisation.

This cost estimate assumes that the data and documentation in this collection has been prepared in accordance with our Instructions for Depositors and is accompanied by appropriate metadata. Changes to data after submission may incur additional charges as will requests for special collection interface requirements. If you have any queries or questions regarding these costs please contact us.

docs.title: Discovery Metadata
docs.text:
This page of the deposition is designed to gather additional metadata that will make it easier for others to find your dataset, via platforms like the ADS Data Catalogue or HSDS Data Catalogue, and what it contains.

Some of this information is collected via the keywords on the Projects page. However, unlike the controlled vocabularies found there, the Discovery Metadata page allows you to enter custom tags and keywords that reflect your collection.

docs.title: Descriptive Tags
docs.text:
Please enter any tags that describe your collection or its content. These tags could relate to the themes of the collection, the methods that you used to undertake your fieldwork and/or research, or anything else that you think is important or unique.

Some examples could be:

To enter a tag, type into the field and press enter. The tag will now appear within a small grey bubble. To remove the tag, click the ‘x’ next to the relevant word.

docs.title: Period Dates
docs.text:
Please enter a start and end date of the historical period that covers the contents of your entire collection. If years in prehistory (i.e. before BC), please enter a ‘-’ sign before the number. Period names (e.g. the Bronze Age, the Roman period) are defined alongside Keywords in the Project pages.

An example could be:

docs.title: Person Keywords
docs.text:
Please enter any keywords of any historical or modern people that are connected to your collection. This could include people who are the subject or focus of the archive, such as materials from a famous archaeologist; people who are depicted in the resource, such as a statue of a historical figure; creators or designers of objects or places within the archive, such as an engineer who designed a bridge; or people associated with events or locations depicted in the archive, such as a notable person who inhabited a building.

This would likely not include people involved in your project - this data is collected on the Project and People and Organisations pages.

Some examples could be:

To enter a keyword, type into the field and press enter. The tag will now appear within a small grey bubble. To remove the keyword, click the ‘x’ next to the relevant word.

docs.title: Location Keywords
docs.text:
Please enter any keywords that describe the geographic location of your project or collection. These keywords are usually terms that would extend the information collected on the Collection Information, Project, or Location pages. They could include a specific place name, site or name of a building.

Some examples could be:

To enter a keyword, type into the field and press enter. The tag will now appear within a small grey bubble. To remove the keyword, click the ‘x’ next to the relevant word.

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
This page is where you upload the data and metadata that forms your collection. Download the Core Metadata template and fill in a row for each digital object.

A digital object can be a single file or a group of files relating to a single object, such as a 3D model or a GIS shapefile.

Further information about how to complete the Core Metadata Template, can be found in the Metadata section.

Step 1 - Upload Core Metadata Template

Begin by downloading the Core Metadata Template using the link provided. All of the metadata for the data in your collection can be compiled within this single and easy to use template.

The template contains some example entries as well as additional information to help you complete the necessary fields. For more details about how to complete the Metadata template, please view the Core Metadata template guidance.

Once your metadata template has been completed, click the ‘Select File’ button and navigate to the location of the template on your computer. After you upload your template the system will automatically review your file and identify if there are any errors.

The errors will describe what information is missing or incorrect and how to rectify the issue. If you have multiple errors you can click the ‘Show more’ and 'Show all’ buttons to expand the list.

You must rectify your errors and re-upload the Core Metadata template before you can continue.

Once you have rectified any errors, or if your metadata template file does not have any errors, then you will be shown a summary.

The summary includes:

  • The name of the file
  • When it was first uploaded
  • When it was last appended to
  • How many total records have been uploaded to the system.

At this stage, the metadata uploaded can be amended in two ways.

Press ‘Replace’ to replace the metadata template with an alternative or updated version.

Press ‘Add Rows’ to select an additional core metadata template that contains additional metadata information. Please note that if this file contains the same information as the first template uploaded, then the system will highlight these duplicates as errors.

You can then proceed to the next step.

Step 2 - Upload Files

Once your metadata template is complete and uploaded to the system, you can now start to upload the files that you would like to deposit.

To do this drag and drop a folder containing your files into the 'Drop Folder Here' box. Please note that the folder must not be compressed in any way (e.g. zipped) to upload it to the system.

As you add the data, the system will process the files and automatically highlight any issues. These may be mismatches between the files you have deposited and the core metadata template. To navigate these issues you can click on the underlined errors and it will expand the list to show all of the files that are affected.

To rectify these issues you have two options:

  • Click Fix Issues - This button takes you to a separate page where you can rectify any specific issues per item. On this page you will be able to add new files, remove rows from the metadata template, as well as other options, to rectify any issues.
  • Click ‘Re-upload files’ - This button will take you back to the start of this step and ask you drag and drop a folder containing your files into the 'Drop Folder Here' box.

Once all of the errors have been rectified you will be shown a summary of the files deposited. You can examine your files by clicking the ‘Browse Files’ button under the summary. On the ‘Browse Files’ window you can navigate through your files and download a file list.

You can then proceed to the next step.

Step 3 - Interface Options

Once the metadata and files that comprise your collection have been uploaded and verified, you can now customise the appearance of your collection when it is published.

Introduction Page Image - Please select a picture from the files deposited that will appear on the Introduction Page.

Overview Page View - Please select a picture from the files deposited that will appear on the Overview Page. Please note that if you did not fill in the Collection Overview section on the Collection Information page, please do not select a photo in this dialogue box.

Select your preferred download page structure - Please select the way that you would like your data to be structured on the download page. The default option is by data category. You can also determine a specific structure that matches the folder structure of the data uploaded as part of your deposit. We recommend no more than three folder levels if possible. We can not guarantee an exact replication of your uploaded folder structure and may need to contact you to discuss your preferences further.

Please indicate if you are interested in discussing a special collection interface - If you would like to create a special collection interface for your collection, please select yes and an archivist will be in touch with you to discuss your options. A special collection interface is a bespoke interface that reflects the specific structure of your data and could include additional features such as a search query or an interactive map interface. This option would incur further costs.

Once you have selected these preferences, your file upload has been completed and you can navigate to the next page.

docs.title: Submitting a New Collection
docs.text:
To submit a new collection for deposition click the green ‘New Collection’ button at the top of the home page.

Navigate through the Collection menu using the tabs on the left hand side of the screen.

You can save your progress using the green ‘Save’ button on the right hand side of the screen. Once all mandatory fields are complete you can also submit your collection using the purple ‘Submit’ button on the right hand side of the page.

Once you have completed the Collection Title on the Collection Information page, the title will appear above the 'Save' and 'Submit' buttons.

docs.title: OASIS Projects
docs.text:
If you have already submitted information about your project to OASIS you can link to this existing information and automatically pull details into Ingest.

OASIS is the online system for reporting investigations into the historic environment and linking research outputs and archives.

To import details from OASIS, enter the OASIS ID into the pop-up box that appears once you hit the green ‘New Collection’ button.

You can also pull details from multiple OASIS projects by entering a comma separated list of OASIS IDs.

If there are no associated OASIS projects with your deposit, you can leave this field blank and just hit Continue.

You can also import data from OASIS later via the Projects page.

docs.title: Invoice Details
docs.text:
This page allows you to enter the details associated with invoicing for the cost of your deposition. You should enter the contact details of a person who is responsible for ensuring the payment of invoices so as to not delay the deposition and publication of your collection.

Default Invoice Details can be updated via the Settings menu. This ensures that you do not have to re-enter invoice details for every collection. The default options can be changed within this page if required.

docs.title: Purchase Order Number
docs.text: If relevant, please enter a Purchase Order number from your organisation to associate with the invoice. Our Finance team will include this information on invoices sent to your nominated contact.

docs.title: Name (first and last name)
docs.text: Please enter the first and last name for your nominated contact. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Organisation
docs.text: If applicable, please include the name of your organisation.

docs.title: Email
docs.text: Please enter the email address for your nominated contact. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Address
docs.text: Please enter the physical address associated with your organisation or nominated contact. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Additional Details
docs.text: This field allows you to provide extra details regarding the invoicing for this transaction. You may include special instructions or any notes that will assist in processing the invoice effectively.

docs.title: Location
docs.text: This menu allows you to provide the geographic location for your project(s). To do this you can use the intuitive map interface and the tools found within the map.

docs.title: Searching the Map
docs.text:
Above the map, there are a number of ways in which you can find the location of your project:

  • Search for a location - Type in a place name to focus your search.
  • British National Grid reference - Use the grid tile letters followed by a full six or eight numeric grid reference (e.g. NZ250638) to focus your search.
  • Eastings and Northings - Use the British National grid reference, separated into a six or eight figure easting and northing (e.g. 425034 , 563870), to focus your search.
  • Latitude and Longitude - Use the World Geodetic System 1984 Reference System (e.g. 54.968824 , -1.6104931), to focus your search.

Using any of the methods above will centre the map of the desired location but will not yet select this location for your project. Zoom and pan in the map window to move your desired location.

docs.title: Selecting a location
docs.text:
To select a geographic location you can use one of three tools:

Tool Description Icon Draw a polygon Use this option to create a bespoke shape for your project area. Click to create a series of points and click the first point to close the shape. Clicking on this option opens a small menu that allows you to ‘Finish’ the shape, ‘Delete the last point’ and ‘Cancel’ creating the polygon. Draw a rectangle Use this option to create a rectangular project area. Click once on the map to start drawing the rectangle. The first point will denote the top left hand corner of the rectangle and the second the bottom right hand corner. Clicking on this option opens a small menu that allows you to ‘Cancel’ creating the rectangle. Draw a marker Use this option to create a single marker for your project. Click on the map once to place your marker. Clicking on this option opens a small menu that allows you to ‘Cancel’ creating the marker.

docs.title: Editing your locations
docs.text:
Once you have created a polygon, rectangle or marker you are able to edit these points using the ‘Edit layers’ and ‘Delete layers’ tools.

Tool Description Icon Edit layers Click on this tool to edit any existing layers. Once you click this tool all existing layers will be highlighted. Click and drag existing nodes to change the shape of a polygon or rectangle or move the location of a marker. Clicking on this option opens a small menu that allows you to ‘Cancel’ any edits. Make sure to hit ‘Save’ after you finish editing these layers. Delete layers To remove any of these layers, click on this tool and then select any layers to delete them entirely. Clicking on this option opens a small menu that allows you to ‘Cancel’ any edits, ‘Save’ any edits that you have made or ‘Clear all’ to remove all layers that currently exist on the map.

docs.title: Allocating a location to a Project
docs.text:
When you allocate a point or polygon on the map, the system will ask which project this location belongs to. Select the relevant project from the drop down list.

This feature allows you to assign a spatial reference to each individual project. If you do not assign a spatial reference to your project then no coordinates will be assigned to your collection in the ADS or HSDS.

docs.title: People & Organisations
docs.text:
This page allows you to enter details about the People & Organisations that are associated with this collection. This includes those who hold copyright for the collection, those who created it and those who funded it. This page also allows you to enter details about who to contact if there are any questions about your collection. Some of these details will appear on your published collection page.

Some fields in the People & Organisations page can be pre-populated with information provided in the Settings menu including Default Copyright Holder and Default Web Contact. This ensures that you do not have to re-enter these details for every collection. The default options can be edited within this page if required.

docs.title: Copyright Holders
docs.text:
Please enter details about who is the copyright holder(s) of the collection. This may include the original author(s) of the collection, or the organisation who is responsible for creating the content. If you are working on collaborative projects across different organisations, it is possible to include multiple copyright holders using this menu.

To add details about the copyright holder first select the ‘Type’ - either a Person or Organisation.

If you selected a Person you will to complete the following fields:

  • First Name
  • Last Name
  • ORCID - The sixteen digit reference number associated Open Researcher and Contributor ID (ORCiD)

If you selected Organisation you will only need to enter the Organisation name. Type in the field to search for available options. If the organisation that you are looking for does not appear, please type in the name manually.

You can select more than one option, either another Person or Organisation, by again clicking the “+ Add Copyright Holder" button. To remove an option, click the delete icon on the right hand side of the entry.

docs.title: Data Creators
docs.text:
Please enter the names of individuals or organisations who created the data in the collection. This could represent multiple people or organisations. They could also include fieldworkers, researchers or personnel involved in data entry.

To add details about the data creator first select the ‘Type’ - either a Person or *Organisation.

If you selected a Person you will to complete the following fields:

  • First Name
  • Last Name
  • ORCID - The sixteen digit reference number associated Open Researcher and Contributor ID (ORCiD)

If you selected ‘Organisation’ you will only need to enter the Organisation name. Type in the field to search for available options. If the organisation that you are looking for does not appear, please type in the name manually.

You can select more than one option by again clicking the “+ Add Data Creator" button. To remove an option, click the delete icon on the right hand side of the entry.

docs.title: Project Funders
docs.text:
Please enter details of the organisation(s) who funded the work or research that resulted in this collection.

To add a Project Funder, click the “+ Add Project Funder" button. Type under ‘Organisation Name’ to search available options. If the organisation that you are looking for does not appear, please type in the name manually.

You can select more than one option by again clicking the “+ Project Funder" button. To remove an option, click the delete icon on the right hand side of the entry.

docs.title: Primary Contact
docs.text: Please enter the name and email address of the person who is the primary contact should an archivist need to contact you about your collection. These contact details will not be made public once the collection is published.

docs.title: Web contact
docs.text:
Please enter information about who users should contact if they have a question about your collection. This name and email address will be publicly available alongside your collection when it is published. A Web Contact can be a Person or an Organisation.

If you are depositing on behalf of an organisation, you may wish to include a general email address rather than the contact details of a specific person.

Please note, if you do enter the details of a specific person under web contact, please ensure you receive their permission to put their contact details on our website prior to submitting your collection.

To add details about the Web Contact first select the ‘Type’ - either a Person or *Organisation.

If you selected Person you will need to complete the following fields:

  • First Name - a mandatory field
  • Last Name - a mandatory field
  • ORCID - The sixteen digit reference number associated Open Researcher and Contributor ID (ORCiD)
  • Email address

If you selected Organisation, you will need to complete the following fields:

  • Organisation Name - Type in the field to search available options. If the organisation that you are looking for does not appear, please type in the name manually.
  • Email address

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
This page of the deposition is designed to gather information about the Project or Projects that form part of the overall Collection. A project(s) is the different facets within a collection - they could represent different phases of a fieldwork project, different stages of a research project or different aspects of a community archaeology project.

For more information about the difference between Collection and Projects please read the How you should structure your collection section.

The structure of your Collection into different Projects can inform the overall structure of your Collection into different sub-sections as it appears on the ADS or HSDS website.

docs.title: Importing from OASIS
docs.text:
Just in the same way as you can import OASIS projects when you start a ‘New Collection’, you can also achieve this via the Projects page. Importing information from OASIS is useful to save you time re-entering information and to ensure that the fields in these different databases align with one another.

To import from OASIS, click the ‘+ Add Project’ tab at the top of the page. A pop up menu will appear asking if you want to ‘Import from OASIS’. Click Yes and input the OASIS project ID.

Relevant details from the OASIS project will automatically populate fields in this menu and a notification will appear at the top of the page.

Information imported from OASIS can be amended in the Project menu, however, we would recommend returning to OASIS to update any entries prior to importing your data. This is because any changes made in Ingest will not be reflected in OASIS and as such the records will not correspond with one another.

docs.title: Multiple Projects
docs.text:
Multiple projects can be added under a single collection. To add a second project scroll to the top of the page and click the “+ Add Project” button. This will create a new menu, which can be accessed as a second tab along the top of the Projects menu. For each new project you can import a OASIS project (as outlined above) or add details manually.

If you created too many projects or would like to remove a project, scroll to the bottom of the page and click the red ‘Remove Project’ button. Please note that once a project has been removed, you will not be able to recover this information.

For more information about the relationship between Collections and Projects please read How you should structure your collection.

docs.title: Project Name
docs.text: Please provide a name for your project. Make sure the name of the project is clear and properly describes the activities undertaken within that project. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Activities
docs.text: Using the dropdown list provided, please describe the activities or tasks that were undertaken as part of this project. Type in the activity into the bar to search available options. You can select more than one option. To remove an option, click the ‘x’ next to the name of the activity. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Project Description
docs.text: Include a description of your project including key objectives, methodology, and significance. You may also include details about the scope of work, fieldwork dates, and expected outcomes. This description should relate to this particular project within your Collection and should not repeat the information from ‘Collection Description' on the Collection Information page. If you have only a single project within your collection, this field may provide more contextual information about what work or research was undertaken that led to the creation of this Collection. This field allows you to enter a maximum of 20,000 characters.

docs.title: Fieldwork Start Date
docs.text: Enter the date when the information-gathering phase of the project began. This may coincide with the start of fieldwork or a research project. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Fieldwork  End Date
docs.text: Enter the date when the information-gathering phase of the project was completed. This may coincide with the end of fieldwork or a research project. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Project Identifiers
docs.text:
Please add any unique Identifiers that relate to your project. This allows us to link your collection to other databases and/or a physical archive.

To add a Project Identifier, click the “+ Project Identifier," button and fill in the following:

  • Project Identifier Type - From the drop down list select the appropriate identifier type, such as site code or museum accession identifier.
  • Project Identifier - Type in the identifier that relates to the specific Identifier Type selected above.

docs.title: Keywords
docs.text:
This menu allows you to add Keywords that reflect your project’s key research findings or subjects. Keywords are important as they help us to make your Collection as findable and accessible as possible via our Data Catalogues and other catalogues.

To add a Keyword, click the “+ Keyword" button and fill in the following:

  • Subject Keyword Type - From the drop down list select the appropriate thesaurus of subject keywords to search based on the monument or objects you are describing and its location within the UK.
  • Subject Keyword - Search for your subject keyword. Type in a keyword and this menu will filter to the appropriate option. Based on the Subject Keyword Type, this menu will search the appropriate thesaurus of subject keywords.
  • Period Keyword Type - Pair your keyword with the relevant time period. Select the location within the UK to search appropriate thesaurus of period keywords.
  • Period Keyword - Search for your subject keyword. Type in a period and this menu will filter to the appropriate option. Based on the Period Keyword Type, this menu will search the appropriate list of periods within the UK.

This is a mandatory field. Please ensure at least one keyword has been added but please add as many as is necessary to sufficiently describe your project.

docs.title: Other
docs.text:
If you cannot find a suitable keyword in either ‘Subject Keyword Type’ or ‘Period Keyword Type’, there is an option to choose ‘Other’. With this option you can manually type in a subject or period keyword. This option should only be used if a suitable alternative cannot be found in the listed thesauri. If you are manually typing in a keyword please ensure that this is directly relevant to your project.

If you have imported your Project from OASIS, any keywords completed in OASIS would have been autofilled for you. This information can be amended in this menu, however, we would recommend returning to OASIS to update any entries prior to importing your data. This is because any changes made in Ingest will not be reflected in OASIS and as such the records will not correspond with one another.

docs.title: Related Projects
docs.text:
This menu allows you to include information about any complementary or associated projects. To add a Related Project, click the “+ Related Project" button and fill in the following:

  • Relationship Type - From the drop down list select whether it is ‘Part of’ or ‘Related’ to your project.
  • Project Identifier Type - From the drop down list select whether the Project identifier is sourced from ‘OASIS’ or ‘Other’.
  • Project Identifier - The identifier by which the related project is known by (e.g. somear1-511984, SJB26).
  • Project Name - The name by which this related project is known by (e.g. A Field Evaluation at London Way, Middlesbrough, RS1 4JK).

docs.title: Reason for Project
docs.text: Using the dropdown list provided, please select the primary reason for undertaking the project. This could be for Planning purposes, as part of a developer-funded project, or as part of a Research project, among other options. If none of the options relate to your project, please select ‘Other’.

docs.title: Related Resources
docs.text:
This page allows you to provide details of any resources that are related to your collection. This could include publications, physical archives, other digital archives or other online resources. These links will appear alongside your collection once it is published and will allow users to find these resources from our digital repository.

To add a Related Resource, click the “+ Related Resource" button. There are three mandatory fields to complete for each resource:

docs.title: Title
docs.text: This is the title of the associated resource. For a publication or other archive, this could be the overall title of that resource. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Resource Identifier Type
docs.text: From the drop down list, select one of the resource identifier options, such as DOI (Digital Object Identifier) or URL (uniform resource location, otherwise known as a weblink). The actual link to that resource will be included in the Resource Identifier field. This is a mandatory field.

docs.title: Resource Identifier
docs.text:
Please enter the identifier for your related resource. This could be a web link (URL) or Digital Object Identifier. Please ensure that the identifier matches with the type listed in the Resource Identifier Type. This is a mandatory field.

Some examples could include:

  • DOI - e.g. https://doi.org/10.5284/1234567
  • URL - e.g. https://archaeologydataservice.ac.uk/archives/1234567/metadata.cfm
  • ISBN - e.g. 990-0-12-123456-1

docs.title: Submitting a Collection
docs.text: Once all mandatory fields are complete, and you have uploaded the Core Metadata Template and files, you can submit your collection using the purple ‘Submit’ button on the right hand side of the page.

docs.title: Actions Required
docs.text:
The Ingest system will then validate all the inputted information and let you know if additional actions are required.

If so, click 'Ok' and navigate through the Collection menu on the left hand side of the screen. Menus highlighted in red require some attention. Within each menu, fields that require action will also be highlighted in red.

Once all of the actions have been addressed, again click the purple ‘Submit’ button on the right hand side of the page to submit your collection.

If all actions have been addressed, a warning will be displayed to remind you that once the collection has been submitted you will no longer be able further changes.

Please Note

If you need to add or edit any information in your collection after it has been submitted you will need to contact the Archivist working on your collection. You can do this using the Messages menu.

docs.title: Deposit Agreement
docs.text:
Once the collection has been submitted you will be asked to read and sign the online Deposit Agreement.

There are two options:

  • Sign the agreement yourself by clicking the green button stating 'I accept the Deposit Agreement'
  • Send the Agreement to a nominated person in your organisation by clicking the purple button stating 'I do not accept the Deposit Agreement and want to send the Deposit Agreement to another individual'

If you choose the second option, a new menu will appear where you will have to complete the following information:

  • Recipient name - mandatory field
  • Recipient email - mandatory field

Click 'Send' to send a the deposit agreement to this selected individual.

docs.title: Email Confirmation
docs.text:
Once the Deposit Agreement has been signed, either by yourself or another individual, the signee will receive a confirmation email providing a copy of the following information:

  • A copy of the Deposit Agreement (as a pdf file)
  • A deposit receipt, providing a list of all the files deposited (as a csv file)
  • A copy of the Cost Estimate creating using the Cost menu (as a pdf file)

Congratulations, your collection has now been submitted!

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text:
All ADS and HSDS Training events are subject to a Code of Conduct.

We are committed to creating a friendly, respectful and harassment-free experience for learning.

We encourage all participants (instructor and learners) to act in the following manner and show respect and courtesy to others at all times.

  • Use welcoming and inclusive language
  • Be respectful of different viewpoints and experiences
  • Show courtesy and respect towards other participants

If you have any issues please report it to your instructor (via Zoom Direct Message or email) and we will address it as a matter of priority. If you have any complaints please contact the ADS helpdesk (help@archaeologydataservice.ac.uk) and we ensure to address all issues.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This section gives you everything you need to get up and running with Ingest and deposit your data with confidence. Find information about live training sessions, step-by-step guidance, and ongoing drop-in support.

docs.title: Where Do I Start?
docs.text:
If you're new to Ingest, try some of these following steps:

Read through our Online Guidance Start at the beginning of our Online Guidance and work through step by step, building from basic concepts to more advanced features, 👉 View Getting Started Pages

Attend an Introduction to Ingest session Our live online training session walks you through the system step by step, including a live demonstration and hands-on practice. 👉 View more information and register below 👇

Try a practice deposit Use our training dataset to complete a practice deposit before working with your own data. 👉 Download our training dataset

Get further help at a Q&A Drop-in session Once you've completed basic training, join one of our fortnightly drop-in sessions to ask questions specific to your datasets and workflows. 👉 View more information and register below 👇

docs.title: Introduction to Ingest
docs.text:
A 1.5-hour online training session for new users. Pre-registration required.

Date Day Registration 7 May 2026 Thursday Register 28 May 2026 Thursday Register 18 June 2026 Thursday Register 9 July 2026 Thursday Register

👉 View further information on the 'Introduction to Ingest' webpage

docs.title: Ingest Q&A Drop-in Sessions
docs.text:
A 1-hour open Q&A for users who have completed basic training. Pre-registration required.

Date Day Registration 14 May 2026 Thursday Register 4 June 2026 Thursday Register 25 June 2026 Thursday Register 16 July 2026 Thursday Register

👉 View further information on the 'Q&A Drop-in Session' webpage

docs.title: Not Sure Where to Start?
docs.text:
If you're not sure which resource is right for you, get in touch and we'll point you in the right direction.

📧 help@archaeologydataservice.ac.uk

docs.title: Ingest Q & A Drop-in Sessions
docs.text:
Drop-in sessions are open, informal Q&A sessions for Ingest users. Come with your questions — whether about your own datasets, your organisation's workflows, or anything you weren't sure about after training. There are no presentations or set agenda; the session is led entirely by what you need.

💡 New to Ingest? We recommend attending an Introduction to Ingest training session before joining a drop-in. Drop-ins are designed for users who have completed basic training and want to go deeper with their own datasets.

docs.title: Session Details
docs.text:
💻 Format: Online via Zoom

⏱️ Duration: 1 hour

👥 Audience: Ingest users who have completed the 'Introduction to Ingest' basic training

docs.title: Upcoming Sessions
docs.text:
All sessions are online via Zoom, 10:00 AM–11:00 PM. See the Training Homepage for a full list of upcoming sessions.

What you can ask about:

  • Working with your own datasets and deposits
  • Metadata requirements specific to your project or organisation
  • File formats, validation errors, and technical issues
  • How Ingest connects to other ADS systems (UMA, Archive Interface, Data Catalogue)
  • Anything you weren't sure about after the introductory training

How your questions help others: Common questions and themes raised in drop-in sessions are recorded and fed back into future training sessions and documentation — so your questions help improve the experience for all users.

docs.title: An Introduction to Ingest
docs.text: This online training session introduces ADS and HSDS users to Ingest, our new deposit system for archaeological and heritage data.

docs.title: Session Details
docs.text:
💻 Format: Online via Zoom

⏱️ Duration: 1.5 hours

👥 Audience: All users

docs.title: Upcoming Sessions
docs.text: All sessions are online via Zoom, 10:00 AM–12:00 PM. See the Training Homepage for a full list of upcoming sessions.

docs.title: About This Session
docs.text: This interactive training session will help you understand and confidently use the Ingest system. We'll begin with an overview of what Ingest is and how it fits into the ADS and HSDS ecosystem, followed by a live step-by-step demonstration. You'll then have the opportunity to try the system yourself with support from the facilitators.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
By the end of this session, you will be able to:

  1. Navigate and complete the basic login and registration process for the Ingest system
  2. Understand the strategic role of Ingest within the ADS and HSDS digital infrastructure (including its connections to UMA, Data Deposit, Archive interfaces, and Data Catalogue)
  3. Identify the information required at different stages of the Ingest system, including metadata requirements
  4. Begin using the Ingest system with your own datasets and deposits

docs.title: Session Schedule
docs.text: Time Duration Activity 10:00–10:10 10 mins Introduction & Housekeeping 10:10–10:20 10 mins An Introduction to Ingest (Presentation) 10:20–10:45 25 mins Live Demonstration 10:45–10:50 5 mins Break 10:50–11:10 20 mins Hands-on Practice Session / Q & A 11:10–11:25 10 mins Wrap-up & Resources 11:25–11:30 10 mins Feedback

docs.title: Before You Attend
docs.text:
1. Register or log in via UMA Ingest is accessed through the User Management System (UMA). If you are an existing OASIS user, you can use your current login. New users will need to register before the session. 👉 Register / Log in via UMA

2. Prepare your materials We recommended that you use your own dataset during the hands-on practice to better replicate real-world conditions. However, if you do not have a dataset to hand a training dataset will be provided at the start of the session — no preparation needed.

3. Check your technical setup - A working Zoom connection (camera optional, microphone recommended) - A modern web browser (Chrome or Firefox recommended) - Access to the Ingest system via UMA

docs.title: Your Trainer
docs.text:
Dr Nicky Garland is the Training and Communications Manager at the Archaeology Data Service (ADS). Nicky is an archaeologist with extensive experience of teaching and training in both developer-led archaeology and academia.

The ADS is the leading accredited repository in the UK for archaeology and historic environment data. With over 25 years of experience, the ADS supports research, learning, and teaching with free, high-quality, and dependable digital resources.
